description_long: This object is a long, rectangular greenhouse with a semi-cylindrical, arched roof made of translucent panels, likely polycarbonate or glass, supported by a visible grid-like frame. The structure sits on a solid, slightly raised base, which appears to be made of concrete or a similar material. The roof and side panels are uniformly segmented, giving the greenhouse a modular and modern appearance. The ends of the greenhouse are flat, with one end featuring a small protruding section that may serve as an entrance or vestibule. The overall color scheme is a muted gray, with the translucent panels allowing diffused light to enter the interior. No plants or interior details are visible through the panels, suggesting the greenhouse is either empty or the opacity of the material obscures the view inside.
description: A modern, arched-roof greenhouse with translucent panels and a solid base, featuring a grid-like frame and muted gray coloring.
description_view_0: End-on view showing the arched roof and the flat end wall, with the solid base clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the full length of the greenhouse, the segmented translucent panels, and the supporting frame.
description_view_2: Opposite end view revealing a small protruding section at the base, likely an entrance, and the grid structure of the panels.
description_view_3: Side view similar to view 1, showing the length and modular paneling of the greenhouse.
